mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques for efficient memory management and request handling. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install vLLM using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.17.0

```

> This command adds the vLLM repository with the latest release.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for vLLM to define its custom resources in Kubernetes. This step ensures that the required CRDs are installed.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.17.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command installs the vLLM CRDs.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs vLLM in the specified namespace, allowing you to manage it using Helm.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.17.0

```

> This command installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
This step creates an example vLLM resource to demonstrate how to deploy a model for inference.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 1
EOF

```

> This command creates a vLLM resource for the GPT-3 model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the vLLM resource to ensure it is running and ready to serve requests.

```bash

kubectl get vllms -n vllm

```

> You should see the status of your vLLM resource.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes vLLM from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls vLLM from the specified namespace.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your configurations and data. This step will upgrade vLLM to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get vllms -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.17.0

kubectl get vllms -n vllm

```

> Backup current vLLM configurations.
> This command upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> Verify that the vLLM resource is running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get vllms -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM resource may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the vLLM pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n vllm'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image for vLLM may not be available.

**Fix:**
Ensure the image tag is correct and accessible in your container registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The node does not have enough memory to run the vLLM pod.

**Fix:**
Increase the memory allocation for your nodes or reduce the resource requests for vLLM.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM pod is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod events with 'kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n vllm' for more details.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM resource listed with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get vllms -n vllm'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
